item.title: 让我们谈谈我们的社交媒体摄入量，好吗？ | Let's talk about our social media intake, shall we?
item.description:
艾琳·本辛格

发表于嘿嘿开发社区！ 在互联网上无聊的一天，是吧？ 我在开玩笑——如果你没有听说过，今天是埃隆马斯克完成他漫长而戏剧性的 Twitter 收购的日子。 或者，正如他所说：

我不是在这里争论马斯克收购计划的细节、Twitter 或 Meta 的未来、它们对民主和全球政治的影响等。如果你想聊聊这些事情，请查看@jmfayard在主题。

这就是我很好奇的地方：改变我们的滚动习惯需要什么。

很明显，我们当前的社交媒体格局正在发生变化，从这次备受瞩目的收购到有关Meta（née Facebook）业务状况的报道之间。 放弃的理由太多了！ 多年来，这些平台也确实重新连接了我们的大脑，以渴望它们的模式和奖励——这意味着离开平台或完全退出可能真的非常非常困难。

另一方面，有一些不那么集中、不那么容易上瘾的在线社交和网络平台（你好！你在 dev.to 上！）。 我第一次在那里注册了一个帐户，我一直很开心地拼凑它的工作原理以及我想如何出现在那个空间中。

所以开发人员，这是我的问题：

  • 您在哪些社交媒体平台上花费的时间最多，目的是什么？ 网络、社交、大脑麻木？
  • 您喜欢首选平台的哪些方面？ 你讨厌他们什么？
  • 要对您当前的社交媒体摄入量做出可持续的改变需要什么？

尼山特 | 吠陀开发者 - 一旦暂停，erinposting 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，erinposting 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，erinposting 发布的所有帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 erinposting 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 erinposting：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 erinposting 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Erin Bensinger

Posted on Oct 28

Hey hey DEV Community! Boring day on the internet, huh? /s

I'm kidding — in case you hadn't heard somehow, today is the day that Elon Musk has completed his drawn-out, dramatic purchase of Twitter. Or, as he put it:

I'm not here to argue the finer points of Musk's takeover plan, the future of Twitter or Meta, their impact on democracy and global politics, etc. If you want to chat about those things, check out @jmfayard's post on the topic.

Here's what I am curious about: what it would take to change our scrolling habits.

It's clear that things are changing in our current social media landscape, between this high-profile takeover and reports about the state of business at Meta, née Facebook. There are so many reasons to quit! It's also true that these platforms, over the years, have rewired our brains to crave their patterns and rewards — meaning that leaving platforms or quitting entirely can be really, really hard.

On the other hand, there are less-centralized, less-addictive platforms out there for socializing and networking online (hello! You're on dev.to!). Lots of folks on Twitter are flocking to Mastodon today, too. I made an account there for the first time and I've been having fun piecing together how it works and how I want to show up in that space.

So devs, here are my questions for you:

  • Which social media platforms do you spend the most time on, and for what purposes? Networking, socializing, brain-numbing?
  • What do you enjoy about your preferred platforms? What do you hate about them?
  • What would it take to make a sustainable change to your current social media intake?

Nishant | The Vedic Developer - Jul 19

Once suspended, erinposting will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, erinposting will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by erinposting will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If erinposting is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag erinposting:

item.title: 如何使用 OpenSSL 使用 AES 加密文件 | How to encrypt files with AES using OpenSSL
item.description:
在本文中，我们将讨论OpenSSL ，为什么要使用它，最重要的是，如何使用它。 但是，在我们开始之前：什么是 OpenSSL？

来源：OpenSSL

公钥/私钥对生成、哈希函数、公钥加密、对称密钥加密、数字签名、证书创建等。 该公司已经开发了 20 多年的技术，并被谷歌和亚马逊等软件行业的巨头广泛使用。

什么是AES？


资料来源：SSL 商店

基本上，AES 是一种对称密钥算法，这意味着它在加密/解密期间使用相同的密钥。

像所有分组密码一样，它可以通过一种操作模式转换为流密码（对任意大小的数据进行操作），但这里不是这种情况。 它可以使用 128、192 或 256 位密钥（产生 AES 的 Rijndael 算法允许更多的密钥大小）。

现在我们已经知道 AES 是什么以及它最初是如何工作的，让我们在终端中通过 OpenSSL 访问它的功能。 可以使用以下命令查看支持的密码列表：

openssl list-cipher-commands


Part of the algorithms in the list

这里我选择-aes-26-cbc
对称密钥加密是使用 OpenSSL 的enc操作执行的。
我们将要使用的方法是在发出命令时指定密码。 首先，我在我的桌面上创建了一个名为“open-ssl”的文件夹，我将要加密的文件（图像文件）“vaulttree.jpeg”放在其中。

让我们加密我们的图像

正如我们在上面的截图中看到的，“open_ssl”文件夹只有一个我们要加密的图像文件。 为此，我们使用以下命令：

openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -pass pass:pedroaravena -p -in vaultree.jpeg -out file.enc

我们的图像现在已加密，我们收到了 salt、key 和 IV 值。
现在，在我们的 open-ssl 文件夹中，我们有图像和加密的图像。 加密后的文件名为“enc.file”。

我们使用了很多命令来加密文件。 但是，他们每个人的意思是什么？ 这是一个列表，其中包含对命令每个部分的说明：

让我们解密加密的图像

openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -pass pass:pedroaravena -d -in file.enc -out vaultree_new.jpeg -P

在解密过程之后，我们现在在同一文件夹中看到一个名为“vaulttree_new.jpeg”的新图像。

同样，让我们​​准确理解我们在命令中使用的代码：

注意换行符

在使用 AES 加密时，您会遇到编码器生成带或不带换行符的 base 64 编码数据的情况。 要解决这个可能的问题，您只需在命令行中添加 -A 即可。 所以它应该是这样的：

openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -pass pass:pedroaravena -d -A -in file.enc -out vaultree_new.jpeg -p

现在，让我们在不指定密码标志的情况下进行操作

使用以下命令进行加密过程：

openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -p -in vaultree.jpeg -out file.enc

它将提示您输入密码并进行验证。

以下命令用于解密

openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -d -A -in file.enc -out vaultree_new.jpeg -p

在这里它会询问我们在加密时提供的密码。

在大多数情况下，salt 默认是打开的。 您可以使用-Salt指定它。

您还可以使用 -S 标志指定盐值。 如果您提供盐值，那么您将负责生成适当的盐，并尝试使它们尽可能独特（您必须随机生成它们）。 我们强烈建议您让openssl处理它。

使用salt时，加密数据的前8个字节为salt保留，加密文件时随机生成，解密时从加密文件中读取。
因此，如果您在文本编辑器中打开该file.enc ，您应该会看到如下内容：

结论

很酷吧？ 使用 OpenSSL 加密文件（在此处了解更多信息），但是，如果您想加密整个数据库怎么办？ 不仅如此，让我们假设您想要加密整个数据库并且仍然进行计算和操作加密数据？！

我们的 SDK 与数据库集成，并以功能齐全的方式加密所有数据，从搜索到算术运算，您可以选择要对数据执行的操作，而无需披露。 完全加密的 SQL 在零信任环境中与数据库进行交易。

想了解更多关于我们正在构建的数据库加密革命的信息吗？ 向我们索取免费演示。 关于 Vaultree

在Twitter (@Vaultree)、 LinkedIn 、 Reddit (r/Vaultree) 或dev.to 上关注 Vaultree 。 访问www.vaulttree.com并注册产品演示和我们的时事通讯，以了解最新的产品开发和公司新闻。



原文:

In this article, we will discuss OpenSSL, why to use it ,and most importantly, how to use it. But, before we start: what is OpenSSL?

OpenSSL


Source: OpenSSL

OpenSSL is a program and library that supports lots of different cryptographic operations, some of which are:
Public/private key pair generation, Hash functions, Public key encryption, Symmetric key encryption, Digital signatures, Certificate creation and so on. Each of the operations supported by OpenSSL has a lot of options and functionalities, such as input/output files, algorithm parameters and formats. The OpenSSL implements the TLS / SSL protocols natively in systems and websites. The company has been developing the technology for over 20 years and is widely used by giants in the software industry such as Google and Amazon.

What is AES?


Source: The SSL Store

AES — Advanced Encryption Standard (also known as Rijndael), is a cryptographic primitive intended to compose symmetric encryption (Symmetric Encryption and Asymmetric, read more here) and decryption systems. Basically, the AES is a symmetric-key algorithm, which means it uses the same key during encryption/decryption.

AES cryptography works as a block cipher, that is, it operates on blocks of fixed size (128 bits, or 16 bytes). Like all block ciphers, it can be transformed into a stream cipher (to operate on data of arbitrary size) via one mode of operation, but that is not the case here. It can work with 128, 192 or 256-bit keys (the Rijndael algorithm, which gave rise to AES, allows for more key sizes).

Now that we already know what AES is and how it initially works, let's access its functionalities through OpenSSL in our terminal. The list of supported ciphers can be viewed using the following command:

openssl list-cipher-commands


Part of the algorithms in the list

Here I am choosing -aes-26-cbc
The symmetric key encryption is performed using the enc operation of OpenSSL.
The method we are going to use is going to specify the password while giving a command. First, I created a folder on my Desktop named “open-ssl”, where I put the file which I will encrypt (an image file) “vaultree.jpeg”.

Let's encrypt our image

As we can see in the screenshot above, the folder “open_ssl” has only one image file which we are going to encrypt. And for this purpose, we use the command below:

openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -pass pass:pedroaravena -p -in vaultree.jpeg -out file.enc

Our image is now encrypted and we received the salt, key and IV values.
Now, in our open-ssl folder we have the image and the encrypted one. The encrypted one receives the name "enc.file".

-

We used lots of commands to encrypt the file. But, what does each one of them mean? Here's a list with an explanation of each part of the command:

-aes-256-cbc: the cipher name (symmetric cipher : AES; block to stream conversion: CBC(cipher block chaining))
-pass pass: — to assign the password (here password is pedroaravena)
-P: Print out the salt, key and IV used.
-in file: input file /input file absolute path (in our example: vaultree.jpeg)
-out file: output file /output file absolute path (here file.enc)

Let’s Decrypt the encrypted image

openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -pass pass:pedroaravena -d -in file.enc -out vaultree_new.jpeg -P

After the decryption process, we now see a new image named “vaultree_new.jpeg” in the same folder.

Again, let's understand exactly the codes we used in our command:

-d : Is used to decrypt the input data.
-in file: input file an absolute path (file.enc in our case)
-out file: output file an absolute path (vaultree_new.jpeg in our example)
-P: Print out the salt, key and IV used (just like the information we received before).

Pay attention to the line breaks

While working with AES encryption you face a situation where the encoder produces base 64 encoded data with or without line breaks. To solve this possible problem, you simply add -A to your command line. So it should look like this:

openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -pass pass:pedroaravena -d -A -in file.enc -out vaultree_new.jpeg -p

-A: base64 encode/decode, depending on the encryption flag.
openssl enc --help: for more details and options (for example, some other cipher names, how to specify a salt etc).

Now, let’s do it without specifying a password flag

With the following command for the encryption process:

openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -p -in vaultree.jpeg -out file.enc

It will prompt you to enter a password and verify it.

Following command for decrypt

openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -d -A -in file.enc -out vaultree_new.jpeg -p

Here it will ask the password which we gave while we encrypt.

-nosalt is to not add default salt. In most cases, salt default is on. You can specify it using -Salt.

You can also specify the salt value with the -S flag. If you provide the salt value, then you become responsible for generating proper salts, trying to make them as unique as possible (You have to produce them randomly). We strongly suggest you let openssl handle that.

When the salt is being used, the first eight bytes of the encrypted data are reserved for the salt, it is generated randomly when encrypting a file and read from the encrypted file when it is decrypted.
So if you open that file.enc in a text editor you should see something like this:

Conclusion

Pretty cool, huh? Encrypting files using OpenSSL (Learn more about it here), but, what if you want to encrypt a whole database? And not only that, let's suppose you want to encrypt a whole database and still do computations and manipulate encrypted data?!

Vaultree has developed the technology to encrypt databases and the AES cipher is only one cipher among the several ciphers we support in our SDK. Vaultree's SDK allows you to pick your cipher: AES, DES, 3DES (TripleDES), Blowfish, Twofish, Skipjack, and more, with user-selectable key size: you literally choose what encryption standard fits your needs best.

Our SDK integrates with databases and encrypts all of the data in a fully functional way, from search to arithmetic operations, you choose what you want to do with your data with no need to disclose it. The fully encrypted SQL transacts with the database in a zero-trust environment.

Vaultree has developed the world’s first fully functional data-in-use encryption solution that solves the industry’s fundamental security issue: persistent data encryption, even in the event of a leak. Wanna know more about the database encryption revolution we are building right now? Request a free demo with us. =D

-

About Vaultree

Vaultree’s Encryption-in-use enables businesses of all sizes to process (search and compute) fully end-to-end encrypted data without the need to decrypt. Easy to use and integrate, Vaultree delivers peak performance without compromising security, neutralising the weak spots of traditional encryption or other Privacy Enhancing Technology (PET) based solutions. item.author: opedroaravena

item.title: 你喜欢恐怖电影吗？ 使用 Twilio 构建万圣节项目 | Do you like scary movies? Build a Halloween Project with Twilio
item.description:
万圣节就在拐角处，这意味着是时候准备服装、囤积零食、使用 Twilio 创建有趣的 DIY 项目并想办法吓唬朋友和家人了。

在这篇博文中，我将教您如何使用 Twilio Studio 构建一个令人毛骨悚然的万圣节 IVR（交互式语音响应）项目， Twilio Studio是我们的低代码/无代码应用程序构建器，带有拖放小部件。

构建此程序后，您可以使用它来吓唬您的朋友和家人。 我相信你会得到一些这样的反应：

想亲自尝试一下吗？ 确保您的手机未设置为“请勿打扰”，然后向+1(940) 394-8137发送短信。 但请注意，这很令人毛骨悚然。

这篇博文的结构如下：

  1. 先决条件：继续之前需要的东西
  2. 先决条件

    在开始之前，在继续之前有以下几点很重要：

    在 Studio 中，我们将创建一个Flow ，它代表您构建的工作流。

    要创建一个新流：

    1. 在Twilio 控制台中登录您的 Twilio 帐户。
    2. 导航到控制台中的Studio Flows 部分。
    3. 单击“创建新流”或“流”标题下方的+图标以创建新流。
    4. 为您的流程命名。 对于这个项目，我们来命名它你喜欢恐怖电影吗？ 然后，单击下一步。
    5. 选择从头开始选项。 然后单击下一步。

    现在我们可以通过将Widgets拖放到Canvas上来开始构建我们的项目。

    我们希望我们的项目在用户向我们的 Twilio 电话号码发送短信后给他们打电话。 要构建它，请将Make Outgoing Call小部件拖放到画布上。 然后，从Trigger小部件中， 绘制从Incoming Message到Make Outgoing Call小部件的转换。

    在这个阶段，如果我们发布流程来测试我们的更改，事情不会立即生效。 我们需要配置一个 Twilio 电话号码以连接到此 Studio Flow 。

    导航到Twilio 控制台的活动号码部分，然后单击您的 Twilio 号码。 向下滚动到消息部分，并通过选择下拉选项“Studio Flow”将您的号码配置为何时收到消息。 您将看到另一个下拉菜单出现，您可以在其中选择您想在此号码收到消息时连接到的 Studio Flow，在这种情况下，您喜欢恐怖电影吗？

    在向您刚刚配置的 Twilio 电话号码发送短信时，它现在应该向同一个电话号码拨打电话。

    从这里开始，我们希望我们的程序能够倾听调用者并与他们进行交互。 首先，在我们的 Flow 中，我们可以将另一个 Widget 拖放到 Canvas 上。 让我们使用Gather Input on Call小部件，并从我们的Make Outgoing Call小部件中的Answered选项连接它。

    然后可以将此数据共享给另一个 Widget 以进行其他操作。

    我们期待一些呼叫者的输入，现在我们希望我们的呼叫说“你好”。 我们将使用另一个名为Say/Play的小部件并将其拖放到画布上。 选中该 Widget，在 Widget Inspector Panel中将其重命名为“hello”。 对于我们的转换，我们将从Gather Input On Call小部件的所有三个选项连接到我们的“hello” Say/Play 。

    对于这个项目，我们想要后者。 让我们使用 Twilio 资产上传媒体文件并将其部署到 URL。

    对于我们的用例，我们将上传一个媒体文件，Assets 将通过 HTTPS 提供该文件。 在我们使用资产之前，我们需要创建一个 Twilio 服务，一个用于函数的容器。

    创建一个新服务：

    1. 导航到控制台中的服务部分。
    2. 单击创建服务。
    3. 命名您的服务。 对于这个项目，我们将其命名为do-you-like-scary-movies 。 然后，单击下一步。

    假设您的计算机上已经有一个以令人毛骨悚然的声音说“你好”的音频文件，您可以上传您的文件。 如果您不这样做，我建议您在互联网上搜索类似的文件或使用您自己的录音。

    单击添加+按钮并从下拉菜单中选择上传文件。 浏览您的文件系统并找到您的音频文件。 将可见性更改为Public 。 然后，单击上传开始上传您的文件。

    资产已上传后，将无法通过 URL 立即访问。 这将由资产名称旁边的灰色圆圈表示。 要部署资产（以及服务的其余部分），请单击Deploy All按钮。 短暂的一段时间后，部署将完成，您应该会看到所有已部署资产旁边出现一个绿色复选标记。

    单击资产旁边的 3 个点，这将打开一个上下文菜单。 然后单击复制 URL 。

    复制资产的 URL 后，返回 Studio Flow：你喜欢恐怖电影吗？ 选择我们之前创建的“hello”小部件，然后在说或播放消息或数字下选择播放消息选项。 将我们资产的URL 粘贴到音频文件的 URL 字段中。 单击保存并发布项目。

    在 Call Widget 上使用第二个 Gather Input并将其放在“hello”Widget 之后。

    在第二个Gather Input On Call小部件之后拖放一个Split Based On...小部件。 连接小部件，以便转换从用户按下的键、用户说的东西和无输入流到基于...的拆分。 您的流程现在应该如下所示：

    但首先，它需要引用一个变量或正在测试的值。 将要测试的变量设置为 widgets.gather_2.SpeechResult。 这引用了从第二个Gather Input On Call小部件收集的调用者输入。

    接下来，选择Split Based On...小部件，导航到Transitions选项卡，您可以在其中配置用户条件（拆分您的 Flow）。 单击 + 按钮，将选择设置为 If Value Contains ，然后输入值“who”。

    创建两个额外的说/玩小部件。 将这些小部件之一连接到Split Based On...的No Condition Matches并将另一个连接到If 值包含 who 。 您的流程现在应该如下所示：

    如果用户说出“谁”这个词，我们希望我们的呼叫响应“你告诉我你的名字，我会告诉你我的”。 如果没有匹配，我们希望我们的电话回复“你喜欢恐怖电影吗？”

    与前面的步骤类似，当我们使用 Twilio Assets 上传音频文件时，我们将在此处执行相同的操作。 尝试在线查找这些剪辑或录制您自己的声音以吓唬潜在的呼叫者。

    迭代项目以添加您自己的交互，然后单击发布按钮以发布 Twilio Studio Flow。

    在万圣节玩得开心

    您拥有构建这个诡异项目的工具，现在继续在万圣节用它来吓唬您的朋友吧！ 根据您的喜好自定义它或通过发短信+1 (940) 394-8137使用我的项目。

    非常感谢您的阅读！ 有关其他万圣节主题教程，请在Twilio 博客上搜索“万圣节”。 如果您发现本教程有帮助、有任何疑问或想向我展示您构建的内容，请在线告诉我。 如果您想了解更多关于我的信息，请查看 我的介绍性博客文章。

    推特@anthonyjdella
    领英@anthonydellavecchia
    抖音@anthonyjdella
    中等@anthonyjdella
    电子邮件adelavecchia@twilio.com
    个人网站anthonydellavecchia.com



原文:

Halloween is right around the corner, which means it's time to prepare a costume, stock up on treats, create fun DIY projects with Twilio, and think of ways to scare friends and family.

In this blog post, I will teach you how to build a spooky Halloween IVR (Interactive Voice Response) project using Twilio Studio, our low-code/no-code application builder with drag and drop widgets.

After you’ve built this program, you can use it to scare your friends & family. I’m sure you’ll get some reactions like this:

Want to try it out yourself? Make sure your phone isn’t set to Do Not Disturb, then send a text message to +1(940) 394-8137. But beware, it’s creepy.

This blog post is structured as follows:

  1. Prerequisites: Things you need before continuing
  2. Twilio Studio Flow: Create a Flow, then build our project with drag and drop widgets
  3. Twilio Assets: Upload static files for our Studio Flow
  4. Twilio Studio Widgets: Continue building our project with Studio Widgets

Prerequisites

Before getting started, it’s important to have the following before moving on:

Twilio Studio Flow

Twilio Studio is a visual tool you can use to build communication projects with little or no code. Within Studio, we will create a Flow, which represents workflows that you build.

To create a new Flow:

  1. Log into your Twilio account in the Twilio Console.
  2. Navigate to the Studio Flows section in the Console.
  3. Click Create new Flow or the + icon underneath the Flows heading to create a new Flow.
  4. Name your Flow. For this project, let’s name it Do you like scary movies? Then, click Next.
  5. Select the Start from scratch option. Then click Next.

Now we can start building our project by dragging and dropping Widgets onto the Canvas.

We want our project to make a phone call to the user after they send a text message to our Twilio phone number. To build this, drag and drop the Make Outgoing Call Widget onto the Canvas. Then, from the Trigger Widget, draw the Transition from Incoming Message to the Make Outgoing Call Widget.

At this stage, if we Publish the Flow to test our changes, things wouldn’t work immediately. We’d need to configure a Twilio phone number to connect to this Studio Flow.

Navigate to the Active Numbers section of the Twilio Console and click on your Twilio number. Scroll down to the Messaging section and configure your number to when A Message Comes In, by selecting the dropdown option “Studio Flow”. You'll see another dropdown menu appear where you can select the Studio Flow you'd like to connect to when a message comes in to this number, in this case Do you like scary movies?

When texting the Twilio phone number you just configured, it should now make an outgoing call to the same phone number.

From here, we’d like for our program to listen to the caller and interact with them. To start, within our Flow we can drag and drop another Widget onto the Canvas. Let’s use the Gather Input on Call Widget and have it connect from the Answered option from our Make Outgoing Call Widget.

The Gather Input On Call Widget (which we just used) allows you to gather a user’s input while they are on a call. This data can then be shared to another Widget for other operations.

We expect some caller input, now we want our call to say “hello”. We’ll use another Widget called Say/Play and drag and drop that onto the Canvas. With that Widget selected, rename it to “hello” in the Widget Inspector Panel. For our Transition, we will connect all three options from our Gather Input On Call Widget to our “hello” Say/Play.

Say/Play can say text messages with text-to-speech or play recorded messages from a URL of an audio file. For this project we want the latter. Let’s use Twilio Assets to upload a media file and have it deployed to a URL.

Twilio Assets

Twilio Assets allows you to upload static files to use with Twilio projects. For our use case, we’ll upload a media file and Assets will serve that file over HTTPS. Before we use Assets, we’ll need to create a Twilio Service, a container for Functions.

Create a new Service:

  1. Navigate to the Services section in the Console.
  2. Click Create Service.
  3. Name your Service. For this project, let’s name it do-you-like-scary-movies. Then, click Next.

Assuming you already have an audio file on your computer that says “hello” in a creepy voice, you can upload your file. If you don’t, I recommend searching the interwebs for a similar file or using your own voice recording.

Click the Add+ button and select Upload File from the dropdown menu. Browse your file system and locate your audio file. Change the visibility to Public. Then, click Upload to begin uploading your file.

While the Asset has been uploaded, it will not be immediately accessible via a URL. This will be indicated by the gray circle next to the Asset's name. To deploy the Asset (and the rest of your Service), click the Deploy All button. After a brief period, the deployment will finish, and you should see a green check appear next to all deployed Assets.

Click on the 3 dots next to your Asset, which opens up a context menu. Then click Copy URL.

Twilio Studio Widgets

With our Asset’s URL copied, go back to the Studio Flow: Do you like scary movies? Select the “hello” Widget we created earlier, and under Say or Play Message or Digits select the Play a Message option. Paste the URL of our asset into the URL of Audio File field. Click Save and Publish the project.

Use a second Gather Input on Call Widget and place that after the “hello” Widget.

Drag and drop a Split Based On... Widget after the second Gather Input On Call Widget. Connect the widgets so that the Transition flows from User Pressed Keys, User Said Something, and No Input and into the Split Based On... Your Flow should now look like this:

The Split Based On... Widget allows you to split your Flow based on user conditions. But first, it needs to reference a variable, or value that is being tested. Set the Variable to Test to widgets.gather_2.SpeechResult. This references the caller input gathered from the second Gather Input On Call Widget.

Next, with the Split Based On... Widget selected, navigate to the Transitions tab where you can configure user conditions (which split your Flow). Click the + button, set the selection to If Value Contains, and enter the value “who”.

Create two additional Say/Play Widgets. Connect one of these Widgets to the No Condition Matches of the Split Based On... and connect the other to the If value contains who. Your Flow should now look like this:

If the user says the word “who”, we want our call to respond with “you tell me your name I’ll tell you mine”. If there is no match, we want our call to respond with “do you like scary movies?”

Similar to previous steps, when we used Twilio Assets to upload audio files, we’ll do the same thing here. Try and find those clips online or record your own voice to spook potential callers.

Iterate on the project to add your own interactions, then click the Publish button to publish the Twilio Studio Flow.

Have Fun on Halloween

You have the tools to build this spooky project, now go ahead and use it to scare your friends on Halloween! Customize it to your liking or use my project by texting +1 (940) 394-8137.

Thanks so much for reading! For other Halloween themed tutorials, search for “Halloween” on the Twilio Blog. If you found this tutorial helpful, have any questions, or want to show me what you’ve built, let me know online. And if you want to learn more about me, check out my intro blog post.

Twitter @anthonyjdella
Linkedin @anthonydellavecchia
GitHub @anthonyjdella
TikTok @anthonyjdella
Medium @anthonyjdella
Dev.to @anthonyjdella
Email adellavecchia@twilio.com
Personal Website anthonydellavecchia.com
item.author: anthonyjdella

item.title: 异步行为让你失望了？ | Asynchronous Behavior Got You Down?
item.description:
这是具有特定解决方案的特定问题。 到目前为止，还很模糊。

在 Javascript/React 中，我们经常遇到异步行为——也就是说，我们代码的某些部分先于其他部分运行。 这可能是由于多种原因造成的，例如代码运行和设置状态的滞后以及fetch requests / promises的性质。

我们有办法处理这个问题。

一种方法是使用 useEffect 挂钩。 这可以非常强大地利用。 我遇到的主要实例是当某些用户操作发生并触发 POST 或 PATCH 请求时，前端显示的数据可能需要更新。 如果我们将存储数据的状态放在依赖数组中，它将被触发并再次运行，从而用新的、更新的数据填充我们的前端。

但有时，即便如此，我们的状态设置（存储我们的一些数据并用于在前端显示它）在设置和代码运行时仍然存在滞后。 这可能很糟糕。 例如，如果我们想要映射我们的状态，它应该是一个数据数组，但它尚未设置为所述数组，应用程序将停止工作。 它会出错并说，“restaurants.map 未定义”。 这在英语中的意思是“我们无法映射它！它不是一个数组！” 它不是，至少不是一两秒。

即使使用 useEffect，这种异步行为也可能令人失望。
虽然这不是“最正确”或“有效”的举动，但我什至使用 setTimeOut() 来解释设置状态的延迟。

在这里，我使用了 .find() 方法，它与 .map() 做同样的事情，遍历一个数组，如果它的对象确实不是数组，它会返回相同的上述错误。 它的第一个参数是您要运行的代码，它的第二个参数是您希望它在页面呈现时等待执行的毫秒数。

我想分享的另一个示例是使用 MaterialUi 库 CircularProgress 中的样式化组件的示例。 如果您有兴趣在 React 项目中实现一些内置样式，MaterialUi Docs 大部分都很棒，并提供了清晰的演练。 在我的示例中，“用户”是我们运行其余代码所需的状态，但如果我们还没有用户，由于某种原因，另一个错误或简单的异步行为，希望是后者，因为如果是，这可能会有所帮助。

在这里，我们使用条件逻辑。 如果我们还没有一个真实值的“用户”状态，我们将返回一个加载图标。 然后，当有用户时，其余代码将运行！

状态可能很棘手，您可能仍然会遇到问题。

另一个更复杂的使用工具是 React 的更新部分，async/await。 这涉及创建“异步函数”而不是常规的 ole“函数”。 我不打算深入讨论，因为那是一篇博客文章。



原文:

This a specific sort of problem with a specific sort of solution. So far, so vague.

In Javascript/React we often run into asynchronous behavior - that is, certain parts of our code run before others. This could be due to a number of reasons, such as a lag in the code running and state being set and the nature of fetch requests/promises.

There's ways we can handle this.

One way is to use the useEffect hook. Whatever code is contained within the useEffect hook will run upon page render AND when a change is made to whatever is in the dependency array.

This can be utilized very powerfully. The primary instance I have run into is when some user action happens and triggers a POST or PATCH request, the data displayed on the frontend may need to be updated. If we put the state, where we stored our data, in the dependency array, it will get triggered and run again, thus populating our frontend with new, updated data.

But sometimes, even still, there is a lag in the setting of our state(s) (which is storing some of our data and being used to display it on the frontend) on being set and the code running. This can be bad. For example, if we want to map over our state, which should be an array of data, but it's not yet set to said array, the app is going to stop working. It will error out and say, something like, "restaurants.map is Undefined." which, in English, means, "We can't map over that! It's not an array!" And it's not, at least not for another second or two.

Even with useEffect, this async behavior can be a bummer.
While it wasn't the "most correct" or "efficient" move, I even have used setTimeOut() to account for the latency of state being set.

Here, I'm using the .find() method, which does the same sort of thing as .map(), iterates through an array, and would return the same aforementioned error if its object is, indeed, not an array. The setTimout() is an asynchronous function, so it's kind of like fighting fire with fire. Its first argument is the code you want to run and its second argument is the number of milliseconds you want it to wait to execute upon page render.

The other example I would like to share is one which uses a styled component from MaterialUi's library, CircularProgress. MaterialUi Docs are mostly great and provide a clear walkthrough if you are interested in implementing some built-in styles to you React project. CircularProgress is a loading circle, more or less.

In my example, "user" is a state that we need for the rest of the code to run, but if we don't have a user yet, due to whatever reason, another error or simply asynchronous behavior, hopefully the latter, because if it is, this might help.

Here, we are using conditional logic. If we don't yet have a truthy-valued "user" state, we will get a loading icon returned. Then, when there is a user, the rest of the code will run!

State can be tricky and you may still run into issues.

Another, but more complex, tool to use is a newer part of React, async/await. This involves creating an "async function" rather than a regular ole "function". I'm not going to go into it because that's a blog post all on it's own.
item.author: jakedapper

item.title: 普通变量 vs 记住 vs 记住 mutabableStateOf 比较 | Normal Variable vs remember vs remember mutabableStateOf Comparisons
item.description:
我们何时以及为什么要在可组合函数中使用记住？

在可组合函数中，您可以通过以下方式声明变量：

  • 正常变量
  • 带remember的变量
  • 究竟有什么区别？

    正常变量

    如果您对countNormal进行更改，则会更新该变量。 但是，它不会触发重组。 如果发生重组（由其他人触发），则执行整行var countNormal = getInitValue() 。

    换句话说，再次调用getInitValue()并将countNormal重置为 0。因此在重组期间countNormal始终为 0。

    记住变量

    它类似于上面的正常变量。 当countRemember更新时，它不会触发重组。 不同之处在于，在重组期间（由其他人触发），它不会执行整行var countRemember = remember { getInitValue() } 。 相反，它获取getInitValue()的缓存版本。

    换句话说，不会调用getInitValue() 。 缓存的值分配给countRemember变量。 因此，该变量在重组期间也总是重置回 0。

    记住 mutableStateOf 变量

    当countRememberMutableState更新时，不仅更新了这个变量，如果有监听器（它正在某个地方使用），它也会触发重组。 与上面的记住变量类似，它获取MutableState的缓存版本，并且不会再次调用mutableStateOf() 。

    由于MutableState对象的这个缓存版本已经更新，该值将不再为 0。 换句话说， countRememberMutableState在重组期间具有更新的值。

    代码示例

    这是一个可组合的函数示例，用于演示我上面解释的内容。

     @Composable fun RememberExample () { var countNormal = getInitValue () var countRemember = remember { getInitValue () } var countRememberMutableState by remember { mutableStateOf ( getInitValue ()) } Column { Text ( text = "countNormal: $countNormal" ) Text ( text = "countRemember: $countRemember" ) Text ( text = "countRememberMutableState: ${countRememberMutableState}" ) Button ( onClick = { ++ countNormal }) { Text ( text = "++countWithoutRemember" ) } Button ( onClick = { ++ countRemember }) { Text ( text = "++countWithRemember" ) } Button ( onClick = { ++ countRememberMutableState }) { Text ( text = "++countWithRememberMutableState.value" ) } } }

    概括

    这是我认为我们应该使用记住的摘要。

    正常变量记住变量记住 mutableStateOf 变量
    如果您不需要缓存变量，请使用此选项如果您的变量计算很昂贵，请使用它，因此您可以缓存它如果您想缓存创建的MutableState对象，请使用它，这也允许您触发重组

    源代码

    最初发布于https://vtsen.hashnode.dev 。



原文:

What is remember in Jetpack Compose? When and why do we want to use remember in a composable function?

In composable function, you can declare variables in the following ways:

  • Normal variable
  • Variable with remember
  • Variable with remember mutableStateOf 
// normal variable 
var countNormal = getInitValue()
// variable with rememer
var countRemember = remember { getInitValue() }
// variable with remember mutableStateOf
var countRememberMutableState by remember { mutableStateOf(getInitValue()) }

getInitValue() is used to demonstrate whether it gets called or executed during recomposition.

fun getInitValue(): Int { Log.d("RememberExample", "getInitValue() is called!") return 0
}

What exactly are the differences?

Normal Variable

If you make a change to countNormal, the variable is updated. However, it won't trigger recomposition. If there is recomposition occurs (triggered by others), this whole line var countNormal = getInitValue() is executed.

In other words, getInitValue() is called again and countNormal is reset back to 0. So countNormal is always 0 during recomposition.

remember Variable

It is similar to normal variable above. WhencountRemember is updated, it won't trigger recomposition. The difference is, during recomposition (triggered by others), it won't execute this whole line var countRemember = remember { getInitValue() }. Instead, it gets the cache version of getInitValue().

In other words, getInitValue() will NOT be called. The cached value is assigned to countRemember variable. Thus, this variable is always reset back to 0 too during recomposition.

remember mutableStateOf Variable

When countRememberMutableState is updated, not only this variable is updated, it also triggers the recomposition IF there are listeners (it is being used somewhere). Similar to remember variable above, it gets the cached version of MutableState and it won't call the mutableStateOf() again.

Since this cached version of MutableStateobject has already been updated, the value won't be 0 anymore. In other words, countRememberMutableState has the updated value during recomposition.

Code Example

This is a composable function example to demonstrate what I explained above.

@Composable
fun RememberExample() { var countNormal = getInitValue() var countRemember = remember { getInitValue() } var countRememberMutableState by remember { mutableStateOf(getInitValue()) } Column { Text(text = "countNormal: $countNormal") Text(text = "countRemember: $countRemember") Text(text = "countRememberMutableState: ${countRememberMutableState}") Button(onClick = { ++countNormal }) { Text(text = "++countWithoutRemember") } Button(onClick = { ++countRemember }) { Text(text = "++countWithRemember") } Button(onClick = { ++countRememberMutableState }) { Text(text = "++countWithRememberMutableState.value") } }
}

Summary

Here is the summary when we should use remember in my opinion.

normal variable remember variable remember mutableStateOf variable
Use this if you don't need to cache the variable Use this if your variable computation is expensive, thus you cache it Use this if you want to cache the created MutableState object which also allows you to trigger recomposition

Source Code

GitHub Repository: Demo_UnderstandComposeConcept

Originally published at https://vtsen.hashnode.dev.
item.author: vtsen

item.title: Python中基于代理的疾病传播模型 | Agent-based disease spread model in Python
item.description:
林纳特·费尔克尔

发表于在本文中，我将演示如何使用基于代理的建模来建模和研究疾病传播。 我将使用 Python 中的abm_framework在 Python 中实现基于代理的 SIR 模型。 此应用程序首次发布在此处：基于代理的 SIR 模型 (Python)

介绍 SIR 模型

下图说明了 SIR 模型。

例如，处于易感状态的任何人都可能被感染。 一旦被感染，您就可以感染处于易感状态的任何人。 可以将感染风险建模为介于 0% 和 100% 之间。

任何被感染的人都会以一定的概率康复。 一旦你康复，你要么抵抗，要么回到易感模式。 每个状态转换都用概率比来描述。

在 Python 中介绍基于代理的建模

基于代理的建模是一种流行的仿真技术，仅次于离散事件仿真、蒙特卡罗仿真和系统动力学建模。

基于代理的模型描述代理及其内部属性和动态，以及代理之间的交互。 希望由此来研究和理解复杂宏观系统行为的出现。

我使用 Python 中的 abm_framework 来实现基于代理的仿真模型。 其设置如下图所示。

它使用 abm_framework 提供的模块。 我以这种方式在 Python 中实现了 SIR 模型。 你可以在这里找到代码。 该模型具有以下规格：5% 初始感染，7% 感染风险，3% 恢复机会。 代理与具有固定半径的已定义社区进行交互。 受感染的代理可以感染该社区范围内的其他代理。

仿真结果

下图总结了使用上一节中描述的参数设置运行的模拟。

恢复和社会距离（有限的感染半径）结束疾病传播。 只有 12% 的特工被感染。


感谢您访问 DEV，我们非常努力地培养这个伟大的社区，并希望您能加入我们。 如果您想创建一个帐户，您可以在这里注册。

萨米娜拉赫曼普尔巴 - 一旦暂停，linnartsf 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，linnartsf 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，linnartsf 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 linnartsf 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 linnartsf 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 linnartsf 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Linnart Felkl

Posted on Oct 28

In this article I will demonstrate how agent-based modeling can be used to model and investigate disease spread. I will use the abm_framework in Python to implement an agent-based SIR model in Python. This application was first published here: Agent-based SIR model (Python)

Introducing the SIR model

The SIR modeling framework is a popular framework for modeling disease spread. SIR stands for Susceptible, Infected, Recovered. Below figure illustrates the SIR model.

For example, anyone in the susceptible state can be infected. Once you are infected you can infect anyone in the susceptible state. The risk of infection can be modelled to be between 0% and 100%.

Anyone infected will recover with a certain probability. Once you are recovered, you are either resistant or you can fall back in to susceptible mode. Every state transition is described with probability ratios.

Introducing agent-based modeling in Python

Agent-based modeling is a popular simulation technique, next to discrete-event simulation, monte-carlo simulation and system dynamics modeling.

Agent-based models describe agents and their inner attributes and dynamics, as well as the interaction between agents. From this one hopes to study and understand the emergence of complex macroscopic system behaviour.

I use the abm_framework in Python for implementing agent-based simulation models. Its setup is illustrated in below figure.

model.py is the application specific model. It consumes the modules provided by the abm_framework. I implemented the SIR model in Python that way. You can find the code here. The model has the following specifications: 5% initial infection, 7% infection risk, 3% recovery chance. Agents interact with a defined neighbourhood, with a fix radius. Infected agents can infect other agents within their limits of that neighbourhood.

Simulation results

Below chart summarizes a simulation run with the parameter settings described in previous section.

Recovery and social distancing (limited infection radius) end disease spread. Only 12% of all agents are infected.

 
Thanks for visiting DEV, we’ve worked really hard to cultivate this great community and would love to have you join us. If you’d like to create an account, you can sign up here.

Samina Rahman Purba - Oct 15

item.title: React FlatifyCSS：扁平化设计的 React 组件库终于稳定了 | React FlatifyCSS: the flat design React component library is finally stable
item.description:
大约一年前，我开始了一个名为FlatifyCSS的项目，它是一个类似于 Bootstrap 的开源 Web 框架，旨在帮助 Web 开发人员创建具有独特设计风格的Duolingo 设计系统的网站。
我在这里写过关于 FlatifyCSS 的文章：

它是如何开始的

在宣布 FlatifyCSS 后不久，我开始为我的个人项目创建 React 组件，结果似乎令人满意，所以我将它作为React FlatifyCSS开源共享。

进展如何

最近，我决定全力以赴并完成它。 事情进展顺利，由于可访问性和性能，我添加了样式组件并重写了一些主要部分。 一些Reach UI基本组件被替换为与 FlatifyCSS 样式一起工作。

主要特点

可定制性

借助 CSS 自定义属性的强大功能，可以自定义颜色、排版、动画等。您可以对每个组件进行自定义，或者通常从配置文件中进行自定义。

可访问性

它对键盘友好，是辅助技术的朋友，并在用户喜欢时支持动画和过渡的减少运动。

支持从右到左的布局

实用的组件集

有 20 多个组件可以混合组成更复杂的用户界面，每个组件都有其独特的 CSS 变量，并且可以根据使用情况进行定制。

基于样式化的组件

组件是基于 styled-components 创建的，因此您可以添加样式并享受使用 styled components 带来的好处。

开始使用 React FlatifyCSS

要安装和使用它，你应该安装flatifycss和react-flatifycss包。 我们使用基本的flatifycss包来导入样式。

安装包

使用 npm 或 Yarn 安装软件包：

 npm i flatifycss react-flatifycss

导入样式

左到右

import ' flatifycss/dist/css/flatify-min.css ' ;

右到左

import ' flatifycss/dist/css/flatify-rtl-min.css ' ;

使用组件！

现在，只需从react-flatifycss导入您需要的组件，例如一个简单的按钮：

 import React from ' react ' ; import { Button } from ' react-flatifycss ' ; export default function App () { return < Button theme = " green " > Success !< /Button> ; }

组件类别

可用组件分为四类：

  • 内容
  • 形式
  • 纽扣
  • 成分

内容

与帖子正文相关的组件，例如图像、引用等。

形式

通常在表单中使用的输入、复选框、单选和其他组件都属于这一类。

纽扣

我记得有人说过：

如果您想知道一个设计系统是否足够好，请先检查它的按钮。

按钮包含多种组件，因此最好将它们与其他组件分开。

成分

手风琴，下拉菜单，模态，吐司和其他东西都在这个类别中呈现。

文件和游乐场

欲了解更多信息，请从这里开始阅读。
The playground is created with Storybook , so you can examine how each component works, read its documents and get a copy of the snippet easily! Playground 是使用Storybook创建的，因此您可以检查每个组件的工作原理、阅读其文档并轻松获取片段的副本！



原文:

About a year ago, I started the project called FlatifyCSS, it is an open-source web framework like Bootstrap to help web developers create websites with the design trend inspired by Duolingo design system, which is unique in its way.
I've written about FlatifyCSS here:

How it started

Shortly after announcing FlatifyCSS, I started to create React components for my personal project and the result seemed satisfying, so I shared it open-source as React FlatifyCSS.

How it is going

Recently, I decided to get my arse in gear and finish it. Things went well, I added styled components and rewrote some major parts because of accessibility and performance. Some of the Reach UI bare-bones components were replaced to work along with FlatifyCSS styles.

Main features

FlatifyCSS has a playful design and gives you features to help you develop your project quickly and easily.

Customizability

With the power of CSS custom properties, it is possible to customize colors, typography, animations, etc. You can do the customization for each component or generally from the config file.

Accessibility

It is keyboard-friendly, the friend of assistive technologies, and supports the reduced motion for animation and transitions when the user prefers.

Support for right to left layouts

FlatifyCSS comes with files prefixed as -rtl.css built especially for the right to left languages like Arabic, Persian, Hebrew, etc. You can also extend the right to left styles by using the flatify-rtl.scss file and conditional statements.

Practical set of components

There are more than twenty components that can be mixed and make more complex user interfaces, each component has its unique CSS variables and can be customized based on the usage.

Based on Styled Components

The components are created based on styled-components, so you can add your styles and get benefits of using styled components with joy.

Start using React FlatifyCSS

To install and use it, you should install both the flatifycss and react-flatifycss packages. We use the base flatifycss package to import styles.

Installing packages

Use npm or Yarn to install the packages:

npm i flatifycss react-flatifycss

Import styles

React FlatifyCSS itself does not include the FlatifyCSS styles, so you can import your preferred stylesheet in your application.

Left to right 

import 'flatifycss/dist/css/flatify-min.css';

Right to Left 

import 'flatifycss/dist/css/flatify-rtl-min.css';

Use components!

Now, just import the component you need from react-flatifycss, for example a simple button:

import React from 'react';
import { Button } from 'react-flatifycss'; export default function App() { return <Button theme="green">Success!</Button>;
}

Component categories

Available components are separated by four categories:

  • Content
  • Forms
  • Buttons
  • Components

Content

Components that are related to post body, things like images, quotes and so on.

Forms

Inputs, checkbox, radio and other components that generally are used in forms are in this category.

Buttons

I remember somebody said:

If you want to know a design system is good enough, check its buttons first.

Buttons include a wide range of components, so it is better to separate them from others.

Components

Accordion, dropdown, modal, toast and other stuff are presented in this category.

Documents and playground

For more information, please start reading from here.
The playground is created with Storybook, so you can examine how each component works, read its documents and get a copy of the snippet easily!
item.author: amir2mi

item.title: 我是如何制作第一个成功的开源项目的 | How I made my first successful open-source project
item.description:
这篇文章最初于 2022 年 10 月 17 日发布到 Medium ，下面列出的统计数据已经针对这篇文章进行了更新

自从我开始我的 Web 开发生涯以来，我可以说我第一次制作了一个成功的开源软件，因为今天我的 TypeScript 包chakra-react-select 的总下载量刚刚超过 100 万。

什么是脉轮反应选择？ 它只是为 React 应用程序制作的标准下拉选择组件，其样式与 UI 组件库Chakra UI相匹配。 然而，这并不完全是我自己的创作。 相反，它是主要流行的React Select的包装器，长期以来，它是GitHub 上最受关注的 React 项目。

以下是一些统计数据，显示了该软件包已经走了多远：

这个项目都是在 2021 年 4 月完成的，当时我开始寻找一个可以与 Chakra UI 一起使用的多选组件，该组件并没有完全偏离它的设计系统。 我想要一个可搜索的下拉菜单，让我可以选择多个状态，或者类似的东西。 我很快意识到我在这个搜索中并不孤单，因为我在他们的GitHub存储库上发现了许多问题，要求为此目的提供一个组件。

人们为此目的制作了一些自定义组件，但它们都不适合我的特定用例。

一些人提到，一种可能性是 Chakra 的创建者简单地包装 React Select，因为它已经是一个高度可定制的成熟包。 然而，他们似乎不会走那条路，而且似乎没有任何时间表让他们实施自己的路线。 所以我想我会试一试。

我从基础开始，只是使用他们出色的样式系统定制了 React Select 的样式，直到它看起来不合适为止。 我对结果非常满意，所以我想我会与 Chakra 社区分享我的创作，作为一个简单的 GitHub Gist ，并附带一个 CodeSandbox 演示。 我很快就开始得到积极的回应，并在要点上进行了相当程度的互动。 从那时起，它已经获得了 30 颗星并被分叉了 10 次。 最重要的是，该演示已被观看 37,800 多次，并被分叉 433 次。

它收到的所有互动都促使我使它变得更好，因此，随着时间的推移，我对要点进行了很多更改以试图改进它。 然而，我很快意识到对要点进行编辑并不是推出更新的好方法。 我认为，在大多数情况下，只有第一次看到它的人才能真正看到这些变化，一旦他们将代码复制到他们的项目中，大多数人可能就不会再看它了。

所以最后，经过 27 次修订后，我决定将其制成NPM 包。 进入它，我知道我会面临一些巨大的挑战。 最明显的是，Chakra UI 的一大亮点是它完全用 TypeScript 编写，这是一种我以前从未使用过的语言。 我知道，如果我想让这个包尽可能有用，那么它就必须用 TypeScript 编写，这样用户才能获得最好的开发者体验。 另一个大问题，也是我没有早点把它做成一个包的主要原因之一，是我知道让人们自定义组件会有困难。

尽管有这些明显的问题，我还是试了一下。 我从非常基本的开始。 我只是希望它以与我的要点相同的方式工作——看不到 TypeScript。 而且，几乎没有制作任何 NPM 包，这需要一些思考。 那天我最终发布了 10 个版本，但都没有成功，所以我决定先放下它，稍后再回来。

一周后（和一些提示），我决定再试一次。 一旦我将使用的构建工具从microbundle更改为rollup （并发布了另外 10 个版本），我就有了一个我满意的工作包。 我在 Chakra UI repo 上的一些相关问题上分享了它，但很快意识到下一步是将整个东西转换为 TypeScript。

对我来说幸运的是，我不是从头开始的。 关于原始要点的大部分评论都是关于添加 TypeScript 支持的，有几个人伸出了援助之手。 考虑到我没有 TypeScript 经验，这些版本极大地帮助了我入门。 三天后，我发布了第一个（半）功能性 TS 版本，但直到几个月后，这些类型才真正正常工作。

不久之后，有人在 repo 上发布了第一个问题。 正如我所预料的那样，它询问的是如何比我已经拥有的更进一步定制样式。 在那之后不久，三个人评论了这个问题，要求同样的事情，所以我知道我必须弄清楚一些事情。 然而，解决方案肯定不会很快到来。

直到三个月后（2021 年 1 月），我终于想出了一个好的解决方案。 为了使其以统一的方式可定制，我必须替换构成 React Select 的每个子组件，以便将它们设置为 Chakra 组件的样式。 最重要的是，我必须与 React Select 自己的并行创建一个全新的样式系统。 这个过程需要我向包中添加大量新代码，但到最后我对它的工作方式非常满意。 下一个要解决的问题是修复 TypeScript 集成。 我还没有意识到这一点，但在此之前的整个时间里，组件上的任何 prop 类型都没有被正确执行。 最重要的是，我注意到react-select@5.0.0 ，它包含内置的 TypeScript 支持，是在我发布我的原始 TS 兼容版本之前发布的，该版本专门用于react-select@4 . 完美的时机对吗？

发布 v2 不到一周后，我有机会坐下来真正深入了解 React Select v5 的类型是如何工作的。 最后我取得了突破，并且通过一些重大的结构更改，我有了一个完全兼容 TypeScript 的版本。 这些更改于 2022 年 1 月 10 日在v3.0.0中发布，我终于确信整个包已经为 Vanilla JS 和 TypeScript 应用程序做好了生产准备。

似乎用户同意它当时已经准备好生产，因为从那里开始每周下载量稳步上升到今天的水平。 我还注意到在回购中提交的问题类型发生了重大变化。 突然间，他们从“为什么我不能用这个包做 X”和“没有一个类型工作”到关于如何实际使用它的简单问题。 当然，在 v3 发布后仍有一些错误需要解决，但它们更多是为了完善它，而不是主要的应用程序破坏问题。 从那以后的大部分时间里，我添加了一些新功能（一些由其他人贡献），充实了文档，并回答了人们提出的问题。

总的来说，制作这个包是一次很棒的体验。 我学到了很多关于不同的构建工具、TypeScript 的工作原理（我现在在我所有的生产应用程序中使用）以及如何支持开源社区的知识。 我还了解了开源维护者在支持一个项目时遇到的困难，例如多次收到相同的问题或被迫告诉人们他们的功能请求无法添加。 但即使考虑到困难，我很高兴我开始了这个项目，我为它取得的进展感到非常自豪！

我会给任何试图进入开源领域的人一些建议；

  1. 找到一个空白，在某个明确需要某物的地方，并填补它。 我过去制作的东西足够独特，没有太多选择，但一开始并没有明确的需求。 在这种情况下，我只制作了这个包，因为我自己需要它，而且碰巧有一个社区正在寻找类似的东西。 正因为如此，我只需在人们要求它的地方链接到它，就可以把我的包裹拿出来。

  2. 当您刚开始时，请尝试回答所有问题，无论它们看起来多么愚蠢。 作为制作软件的人，一些问题对您来说可能看起来微不足道或显而易见，但它们在您的文档中可能不像您想象的那样清楚地描述。 这样做的另一个好处是，您回答的问题/讨论越多，人们在将来提出问题之前必须查看的内容就越多。

  3. 添加到第 2 点 - 如果您发现自己一遍又一遍地遇到相同的问题，请尝试在文档中更多地突出这些内容。 好的文档将节省您解释事情的时间，您的用户将帮助您了解他们需要什么才能使文档更加清晰和完整。

  4. 您不需要为您的文档托管整个网站，尤其是在您的项目不是那么大的情况下。 您的 GitHub 存储库上的 README 可能很多，有时使用旧的 ctrl/cmd + f 更容易找到您要查找的内容。 但是，包括完整的功能示例肯定会有所帮助。 对于 React 组件，我建议链接到CodeSandbox示例，这样人们就可以看到不同的用例并自行修改实现。

在一天结束时，只需开始制作东西！ 除非您将代码放在那里，否则您永远不知道会发生什么，如果您不首先编写代码，就无法将代码放在那里。



原文:

This post was originally published to Medium on Oct 17, 2022 and the stats listed below have been updated for this post

For the first time since I started my career in web development, I can say I’ve made a successful piece of open-source software, because today my TypeScript package chakra-react-select just passed 1 million total downloads.

What is Chakra React Select? It’s just your standard dropdown select component made for React applications, styled to match the UI component library Chakra UI. However, it’s not entirely my own creation. Instead it’s a wrapper for the majorly popular React Select, which, for a long time, was the most starred React project on GitHub.

Here are some stats showing how far the package has come:

This project all came about in April 2021, when I started searching for a multi select component I could use with Chakra UI that didn’t completely diverge from it’s design system. I wanted a searchable dropdown that allowed me to select multiple states, or something along those lines. I quickly realized I was not alone in this search as I found many many issues on their GitHub repo requesting a component for this purpose.

There were a few custom components people had made for this purpose, but none of them fit for my specific use case.

A few people mentioned that one possibility would be for the creators of Chakra to simply wrap React Select, as it is already a mature package that is highly customizable. However, it didn’t seem like they were going to take that route, and there didn’t seem to be any timeline for them implementing their own. So I figured I’d give it a shot.

I started off with the basics and just customized the styles of React Select using their great styling system until it didn’t look out of place. I was pretty happy with the result so I figured I’d share my creation with the Chakra community as a simple GitHub Gist with a CodeSandbox demo to go along with it. I quickly began getting positive responses and a decent level of interaction on the gist. Since then it has received 30 stars and been forked 10 times. On top of that, the demo has been viewed 37,800+ times and been forked 433 times.

All of the interaction it received motivated me to make it better, so, over time, I made quite a few changes to the gist in an attempt to improve it. However, I soon realized that making edits to a gist is not a great way to push out updates. I figured that, for the most part, only people who were seeing it for the first time would actually see those changes and once they copied the code into their projects, most would probably not look at it again.

So finally, 27 revisions on the gist later, I decided to make it into an NPM package. Going into it, there were some big challenges I knew I would face. The most obvious being that one of the big draws of Chakra UI is that it’s written entirely in TypeScript, a language I had never used before. I knew that if I wanted to make the package as useful as I could, then it would have to be written in TypeScript so users would have the best developer experience possible. The other big issue, and one of the main reasons I didn’t make it into a package sooner, was that I knew there would be difficulties in allowing people to customize the component.

Despite these glaring issues I gave it a shot anyway. And on Sep 13, 2021 I made my first commit to the new repo and published the first version to NPM. I started off very basic. I just wanted it to work in the same way my gist did- with no TypeScript in sight. And, having barely made any NPM packages, this took a bit of figuring out. I ended up publishing 10 versions that day, none of which worked, so I decided to put it down for a bit and come back to it later.

After a week (and some prompting), I decided to give it another shot. Once I changed the build tool I was using from microbundle to rollup (and published another 10 versions), I had a working package I was satisfied with. I shared it on a few of the relevant issues on the Chakra UI repo but quickly realized that the next step would be converting the whole thing to TypeScript.

Luckily for me, I wasn’t starting from scratch. A large portion of the comments on the original gist were about adding TypeScript support and a couple people lent a hand. These versions helped tremendously in getting started considering I had no TypeScript experience. I published the first (semi) functional TS version three days later, but it wasn’t until a few months later that the types actually worked properly.

Soon after that someone made the first issue on the repo. As I had anticipated, it was asking about how to customize the styles further than I already had. three people commented on the issue requesting the same thing shortly after that, so I knew I had to figure something out. However, the solution definitely did not come quickly.

It wasn’t until three months later (Jan 2021) that I finally figured out a good solution. In order to make it customizable in a unified way, I had to make a replacement for every sub-component that makes up React Select so they could be styled as Chakra components. On top of that, I had to make an entirely new styling system in parallel to React Select’s own. The process required me to add a ton of new code to the package but by the end of it I was very satisfied with how it worked. On Jan 4, 2022 I released chakra-react-select@2.0.0 and finally put all the customization requests to rest.

The next problem to tackle was fixing the TypeScript integration. I hadn’t realized it, but for the entire time up until this point, none of the prop types on the component were being enforced properly. On top of that, it was brought to my attention that react-select@5.0.0, which included built in TypeScript support was released one day before I released my original TS compatible version that was made to work specifically with react-select@4. Perfect timing right?

Less than a week after releasing v2 I had a chance to sit down and really dig into how the types worked for React Select v5. Finally I had a breakthrough, and with some major structural changes I had a version that was fully TypeScript compatible. These changes were released in v3.0.0 on Jan 10, 2022, and I was finally confident that the whole package was production ready for Vanilla JS and TypeScript applications.

It seems that the users agreed it was production ready at that point because from there on the downloads/week then steadily rose up to the point it’s at today. I also noticed a major change in the types of issues that were being filed on the repo. All of a sudden they went from “why can’t I do X with this package” and “none of the types are working” to simple questions about how to actually use it. There were still some bugs to work through after the release of v3 of course, but they were more about polishing it up than major app breaking issues. For the most part since then, I’ve added a few new features (some contributed by others), fleshed out the documentation, and answered questions as people have had them.

Overall, making this package has been a great experience. I’ve learned a ton about different build tools, how TypeScript works (which I now use in all of my production applications), and how to support an open-source community. I’ve also learned about the struggles open-source maintainers deal with when supporting a project, such as receiving the same question multiple times or being forced to tell people that their feature request can’t be added. But even taking the struggles into account, I’m happy I started this project and I’m very proud of how far it’s come!

There’s a few pieces of advice I would give anyone trying to break into the open-source space;

  1. Find a gap, somewhere there is a clear need for something, and fill it. I’ve made things in the past that have been unique enough to not have many alternatives, but there wasn’t a clear need for them in the first place. In this case I only made this package because I needed it for myself and just so happened to have a community looking for something similar. Because of that, I was able to get my package out there simply by linking to it in those places where people were asking for it.

  2. When you’re just starting off, try and answer all questions, no matter how dumb they might seem. As the person who made the software some questions may seem trivial or obvious to you but they might not be as clearly described in your documentation as you think. An added benefit of this is that the more issues/discussions you answer, the more content people will have to look through before asking questions in the future.

  3. Adding to number 2- if you find yourself getting the same question over and over, try and highlight those things more in your documentation. Good documentation will save you time explaining things down the road, and your users will help you understand what they need for the documentation to be more clear and complete.

  4. You don’t need to host an entire website for your documentation, especially if your project isn’t that big. The README on your GitHub repo can be plenty, and sometimes its easier to find what you’re looking for with a good old ctrl/cmd + f. However, including complete functional examples certainly helps. In the case of React components, I recommend linking to CodeSandbox examples so people can see different use cases in action and tinker with the implementation themselves.

At the end of the day, just start making things! You never know what might take off unless you put your code out there and you can’t put your code out there if you don’t code things in the first place.
item.author: csandman

item.title: YANB：又一个新博客…… | YANB: Yet another new blog...
item.description:
好的...我要开始另一个新博客了。 但为什么？

主要原因之一是我在其他地方的所有旧博客都与开发世界无关。 有一些关于我的小提琴演奏的博客，我非常喜欢。 棒球、游泳、骑车、滑雪等运动生活也是离不开的，所以有空就写博客。 甚至还有关于我在家做饭和在外面用餐的博客。 那些才是我真正可以逃离现实的地方。 不幸的是，我上次更新其中一个是在 5 年前。

那我为什么要开始这个？
因为我想开始记录我在开发世界的生活。

我以前的开发生活很简单：我从高中开始就是一名开发人员。 我在 1998 年编写了我的第一行 Perl 和 Javascript。我在 2001 年作为“开发人员”获得了第一笔收入！ 我在高中时把我的第一款 TI-83 游戏卖给了我的朋友。 从那时起，我知道我将来会用软件做一些事情。 我继续使用 Perl 和 Javascript，然后是 VB、PHP、Objective C、Java、Classic ASP、Swift、Kotlin、Golang、NodeJS、TypeScript for Angular/React 等。而且它不仅限于编程语言。 我在基础设施、DevOps、产品/项目管理、团队负责人、解决方案架构师，甚至是共同创立/创立公司方面都有我的经验！ 我很难在采访中回顾所有这些经历，因为发生的故事，尤其是来自初创公司的故事，可能会持续一个多小时。 但即便如此，一切都很简单：我提高现有技能，学习一两项新技能，然后下班。

去年情况发生了一些变化。 去年，我被一家初创公司聘为工程总监一职，这完全改变了我对许多事情的看法。 影响我多年心态的最重大变化是我不能再介入和编码。 当我是一名高级人员、团队负责人或解决方案架构师时，我已经无法将 100% 的时间用于编码，但我仍然有大约 30% 的时间来亲自动手并处理它们。 然而，作为一名导演，我不能（而且可能不应该）。 这是令人震惊的。

为什么这令人震惊？
因为您无法再清楚地了解为什么特定项目可能需要重新协商优先级或截止日期。

虽然我喜欢软件开发，因为我可能每天可以工作 20 小时（尤其是现在有了 WFH，我可以睡 3 小时 55 分钟！）并且仍然能够在 3 天内完成，但我不能问我的团队去做吧。

这意味着路线图将被推动！ 不仅企业对我的团队无法通过一个简单的要求按时交付感到不满，而且开发人员也不高兴我为什么同意将这么多项目放在路线图上。 这是一个双输的局面。

无论如何，在领导团队中仍然是一个非常有趣的经历。 我可以从不同的角度学习很多古老的东西。 以后，我会在博客上写一点我的导演生活，也会写几篇我会怎么做的文章。 直到下一次...

停产



原文:

Ok... I'm starting another new blog. But why?

One of the main reasons is that all my old blogs elsewhere are not related to the development world. There are blogs about my violin playing, which I'm a big fan of. Sports life like baseball, swimming, biking, and skiing is also something I can't live without, hence a blog when I have time. There are even blogs about my cooking at home and dining outside. Those are truly the place where I can escape from reality. Unfortunately, I last updated one of those 5 years ago.

So why am I starting this one?
Because I want to start documenting my life in the dev world.

NL - New Life

My previous dev life was simple: I've been a developer since high school. I wrote my first line of Perl and Javascript in 1998. I had my first income in 2001 as a "developer"! I sold my first TI-83 game to my friends in high school. From that point on, I know I'll be doing something with software in the future. I continue with Perl and Javascript, then VB, PHP, Objective C, Java, Classic ASP, Swift, Kotlin, Golang, NodeJS, TypeScript for Angular/React... etc. And it's not limited to programming language. I had my experiences in infrastructure, then DevOps, product/project management, team lead, solution architect, and even co-founding/founding a company! It's hard for me to go over all those experiences in an interview because the stories that happened, especially from the startup, can last over an hour. But even that, everything is simple: I sharpen my current skill, learn a new skill or two, and I'm off work.

Things changed a bit last year. I was hired for the position of Director of Engineering for a startup last year, which totally changed my perspective on many things. The most significant change that affects my many years of mindset is that I can no longer step in and code. I am already unable to spend 100% of my time coding when I'm a senior, team lead, or solution architect, but I still get about 30% of my time to get my hands dirty and work on them. However, as a director, I can't (and probably shouldn't). It is alarming.

NBA2C-ALARM - Not being able to code is alarming

Why is this alarming?
Because you can no longer have a clear view of why a particular project might need renegotiation of priorities or deadlines.

"Cliff, this can't be done in 3 days..."

"Why not? That seems easy enough. We only need to add an entry to a table, which should be about an hour. So 3 days are with buffer already..."

"Due to the time constraint, the value was hard-coded the last time we worked on it. We thought it was fine because it's not sensitive information. Unfortunately, it's hard-coded in the backend and hard-coded in the frontend with an enum. We don't even have the table to hold the value now. So now, not only do we need to work on the feature with those extra works, but we need to work on a solution to migrate about 100k data during deployment. We need to take care of the automation tests and everything. I think we need probably close to 2 weeks..."

Although I love software development as I can probably work 20 hours a day (especially now that with WFH, I can sleep a solid 3 hours and 55 minutes!) and still be able to finish in 3 days, I can't ask my team to do it.

This implies that the roadmap will be pushed! Not only are the business not happy that my team can't deliver on time with a simple requirement, but developers are also not happy why I agree with so many items to be put on the roadmap. It's a lose-lose situation.

Anyways, being in the leadership team is still a very fun experiences. I get to learn a lot of old things from a different perspective. In the future, I'll blog a bit of my director life and also write a few articles on how I would do thing. Until next time...

EOL
item.author: snowmen10

item.title: 钛新闻#12 | Titanium News #12
item.description:
迈克尔·甘戈尔夫

发表于较早的帖子可以在这里找到。

介绍

许多模块更新、一些预览和有关下一个 SDK 的新闻。

钛商品

向公众展示您使用最好的移动开发框架😉

我已经建立了一个个人 Spreadshirt 商店，里面有一些 Ti 商品：
将来会增加一些设计。

钛SDK

一些新的 PR 已合并到 Titanium 主分支，并将在下一个版本 12.0.0 中发布。

我们从新的 Android 布局开始。 下一个版本将为您带来 Material3 布局：

  • 当然，您可以使用所有其他 Android 主题。 有了它，您将拥有一个新的辅助函数Ti.UI.Android.harmonizedColor(color) ，它将生成您传入的颜色的“协调颜色”（ PR ）。

    修复了不平衡的视图控制器转换导致错误 ( PR ) 的旧 iOS 问题。

    如果您只使用 rightView 而没有 leftView ( PR )，Android 抽屉事件现在可以正常工作。

    默认模板和文档已更新。

    预习

    新 PR 正在等待审核合并：

    查看https://github.com/tidev/titanium_mobile/pulls以获取完整列表。

    模块

    还有许多模块更新和一些新的检查！

    两项行动将帮助您完成该任务

    我目前正在更新我的 ti.animation 模块以支持Rive动画。 如需预览视频，请查看：
    教程

    添加了一个新教程，以使用 Android 的“不保留活动”设置来查找应用程序中的内存问题。 您可以在从零到应用程序 - Android：使用“不保留活动”开发人员设置查找错误。

    而已

    如果您有反馈或一些您想分享的有趣的 Titanium 应用程序、模块或小部件：与我联系或发表评论，我会将其添加到下一个Titanium news中。

    一旦暂停，miga 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，miga 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，miga 发布的所有帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 miga 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 miga 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

取消标记 miga 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Michael Gangolf

Posted on Oct 28

Older posts can be found here.

Intro

Many module updates, some previews and news about the next SDK.

Titanium merchandise

Show the public that you work with the best mobile dev framework 😉

I've set up a personal Spreadshirt shop with some Ti merchandise:
https://titanium-mobile.myspreadshop.net/
Will add some more designs in the future.

Titanium SDK

A few new PRs have been merged to the Titanium master branch and will make it in the next release version 12.0.0.

We start with new Android layouts. The next release will bring you Material3 layouts:

  • Theme.Titanium.Material3.DayNight
  • Theme.Titanium.Material3.DayNight.NoTitleBar
  • Theme.Titanium.Material3.DayNight.Fullscreen

of course you can use every other Android theme. And with that you will have a new helper function Ti.UI.Android.harmonizedColor(color) that will generate a "harmonizing color" of the color you are passing in (PR).

An old iOS issue was fixed where unbalanced view controller transitions caused errors (PR).

Android drawer events work correctly now if you just use a rightView and no leftView (PR).

A very "hidden" error in Listviews was fixed on Android that could cause a crash when e.g. a HTTP request is completed but the activity is gone already (PR)

The default template and documentation was updated.

Preview

New PRs are waiting for a review to get merged:

Check https://github.com/tidev/titanium_mobile/pulls for a full list.

Modules

And again many module updates and some new ones to check out!

Titanium Freshchat SDK: Use the native Freshchat SDK

https://github.com/hansemannn/titanium-freshchat

Titanium Parse SDK Module
https://github.com/prashantsaini1/ti-parselivequery

Titanium iOS Notification Service Extension An example implementation of using a native iOS Notification Service Extension (to display images in remote push notification) in Titanium.

https://github.com/hansemannn/titanium-notification-service-extension

PurgeTSS Again some big updates to the tailwind class library for Titanium

https://github.com/macCesar/purgeTSS

Github Actions Want to use Github actions to build your Titanium app? Two actions will help you with that task

https://github.com/marketplace/actions/install-titanium-sdk


https://github.com/marketplace/actions/titanium-android-build

Ti.Animation Rive support
I'm currently updating my ti.animation module to support Rive animations. For a preview video have a look at:
https://twitter.com/MichaelGangolf/status/1583137289794097154

Tutorials

Added a new tutorial to find memory issues in your app with Androids "Don't keep activities" setting. You can find it at From zero to app - Android: Find bugs with the “don’t keep activities” developer setting.

That's it

If you have feedback or some interesting Titanium apps, modules or widgets you would like to share: get in contact with me or leave a comment and I'll add it to the next Titanium news.

Once suspended, miga will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, miga will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by miga will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If miga is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag miga:

miga consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging miga will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 每个人都有无服务器！ | There’s Serverless for Everyone!
item.description:
一个周六早上，当你与家人团聚吃早午餐时，你双手捧着两盘牛排离开厨房，大声喊道：“每个人都可以使用 Serverless！”

你的配偶正在吞噬任何东西。 另一方面，艾玛不禁凝视着太空。

我们早餐怎么吃？”

它的创建是为了将照片和对象上传到 Amazon S3、更新 DynamoDB 表、响应网站点击以及响应智能设备上的传感器读数。 无服务器并不意味着没有服务器。 无服务器意味着如果服务器在凌晨 3:54 崩溃，那不是你的问题。

部署：部署一个函数就像在容器中配置它一样简单。 如果函数是具有依赖项的包，则必须先将其压缩并上传到 S3 存储桶。 为了让开发人员可以专注于他们的代码，AWS 会构建容器、对其进行配置和管理。

事件：您编写的函数应设置为触发事件。 它可能是简单的短信或电话、火警或两者兼而有之，但它需要引起刺激。

您无权访问任何可以让您控制云基础设施的基础设施资源，因为无服务器计算有助于抽象它。

  • 涉及的操作：只有当事件触发时，您的代码才会运行，并且只有在运行时的时间跨度内才会付款。 对于希望使用 Lambda 来预置由 HTTP 请求（例如）提示的后端服务的 Lambda 用户来说，此特定功能是有利的，因为它可以在不使用时保留资源。 因此，由于您只需为使用 Lambda 时实际使用的计算时间付费，因此成本几乎不是问题。

    • 除了完全由事件驱动之外，Lambda 还进行自主垂直扩展。 在几毫秒内，它可以扩展到许多并行资源。 缩小过程也会自动处理，一旦完成，活动操作就会被丢弃。

    它用于无服务器网站、日志分析、批量实时数据处理、自动备份、后端清理以及任何其他需要按需使用的用例。

    在以下博客文章中， Ryan Kauffman展示了如何使用 Python、AWS IOT、AWS Lambda 和 TwilioWireless 来挽救蹒跚学步的孩子的生命。

    无服务器是一个概念。”

    这开始有点道理。”

    让我们以布鲁诺为例。 布鲁诺的职责是在我们晚上睡觉时照看我们。 因为布鲁诺是应该赶走小偷的人，所以如果你一夜醒来发现有人试图偷偷溜进这处房产，这不是你的责任。 布鲁诺是 Lambda，因为当我们睡觉时，他会照顾我们。”

    拉胡尔·基兰·加达姆 - 一旦暂停，aws-builder 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，aws-builders 将能够再次评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，aws-builders 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 aws-builders 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 aws-builders：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 aws-builders 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    When you reunite your family for brunch one Saturday morning, you leave the kitchen with two plates of steak in both hands, shouting at the top of your lungs, "There's Serverless for Everyone!”

    Your spouse is devouring whatever. Emma, on the other hand, can't help but stare into space.

    "You're not eating anymore, Emma."

    “What exactly is Serverless, Mom? How come we're having it for breakfast?”

    AWS Lambda is an event-based compute service offered by Amazon Web Services that allows you to run code without controlling or provisioning servers. It was created to upload photos and objects to Amazon S3, update DynamoDB tables, respond to website clicks, as well as react to sensor readings on a smart device. AWS Lambda is essentially a service that runs code in response to events while also automatically managing the compute resources required by that code.

    Serverless does not imply the absence of servers. Serverless means that if a server crashes at 3:54 am, it’s not your problem.

    How Lambda Works

    • The Deployment: Deploying a function is as simple as configuring it in a container. In the event that the function is a package with dependencies, it must first be zipped and uploaded to an S3 bucket. AWS is the step's active character. So that the developer can concentrate on their code, AWS builds the container, configures it, and manages it.

      AWS creates an instance, which is a container made from Amazon Linux AMIs (Amazon Machine Images) that has a configurable runtime of 1–900 seconds, a RAM range of 128MB–10240 MB, and temporary storage ranging from 512MB–10GB.

    • The Event: The function you write should be set to trigger an event. It might be a simple SMS or call, a fire alarm, or both, but it needs to cause stimuli.

      You don't have access to any infrastructure resources that would let you take control of the cloud infrastructure because serverless computing helps to abstract it.

    • The Operations involved: Only when the event is triggered will your code run, and only during the time-span of the runtime will payments be made. This specific feature is advantageous for Lambda users who wish to use Lambda to provision back-end services prompted by HTTP requests (for example), as it preserves resources when not in use. As a result, since you only pay for the compute time you actually use when using Lambda, cost is almost never an issue.

    In addition to being entirely event-driven, Lambda also conducts autonomous vertical scaling. Within milliseconds, it can scale up to many parallel resources. The /tmp folder contains temporal storage, which is only locally accessible for the duration of the instance. The scaling down process is also handled automatically, and active operations are discarded once they are complete.

    AWS Lambda natively supports numerous programming languages and frameworks, and also offers customization options based on uploaded functions. It is used for serverless websites, log analysis, bulk real-time data processing, automated backups, back-end cleaning, and any other use case that calls for on-demand use.

    In the following blog-post, Ryan Kauffman shows how to use Python, AWS IOT, AWS Lambda, and TwilioWireless to save a toddler's life.

    “You still haven't explained what serverless is, mom.”

    “Okay Emma. Serverless is a concept.”

    “What concept?”

    "Serverless is comparable to driving to the mall. Fuel is the only thing you used to arrive there.”

    “Okay. That begins to make some sense.”

    “There’s also this serverless quote: ‘Serverless also means that it's not your problem if a server crashes at two in the morning’. Let's use Bruno as an example. Bruno's duty is to watch after us when we sleep at night. Because Bruno is the one who should be chasing thieves away, it is not your responsibility if you wake up one night and notice one trying to sneak around this property. Bruno is Lambda because while we’re asleep, he takes care of us.”

    Rahul Kiran Gaddam - Sep 22

item.title: 迪拜的乙烯基地板 | Vinyl Floorig in Dubai
item.description:
我们的防水、防刮乙烯基地板将使您的生活轻松自如。 今天的乙烯基地板经久耐用、维护成本低且易于安装。 我们拥有一切，无论您需要豪华乙烯基木板地板、乙烯基瓷砖还是乙烯基板材地板。 我们最受欢迎的款式、颜色、质地和顶级品牌的范围无与伦比。 因为它的经济利益，你会想把你房子的每个房间都放进去。
乙烯基地板迪拜
迪拜乙烯基地板为您提供最好的安装服务，满足您的所有地板要求。 我们为任何商业或住宅环境（如家庭、办公室、酒店、学校、餐厅等）提供最好的乙烯基地板、硬木地板和各种其他地板选项。我们的系列包括：

乙烯基瓷砖 乙烯基板 地板 地脚线 定制订单

我们是设计、耐用性和长期保证的高品质组合，价格实惠。

乙烯基地板是由 4 层材料制成的地板覆盖物。 第一层或底层是背衬层，通常由软木或泡沫构成。 它旨在用作乙烯基地板的垫层，无需在铺设之前安装另一种材料。 它还可以作为垫子，使在地板上行走更加愉快，并作为隔音屏障来防止噪音。

乙烯基地板的类型
乙烯基地板

乙烯基瓷砖看起来像石头或瓷砖。 它们与乙烯基木板一样，有多种设计和颜色可供选择，以反映其天然对应物。 有些人在安装乙烯基瓷砖时使用灌浆来更准确地近似石头或瓷砖的外观。 许多人喜欢在家中的一小部分使用乙烯基瓷砖，因为与石材瓷砖不同，乙烯基瓷砖可以轻松修剪以适应小空间。

乙烯基板

与乙烯基木板和瓷砖不同，乙烯基板可卷成 12 英尺宽，并且可以一步安装。 最好的选择是乙烯基片材，因为它的经济性和耐用性。

乙烯基木板

乙烯基木板旨在提供硬木地板的外观，并有多种木质图案可供选择。 许多人选择乙烯基木板而不是木材，因为乙烯基木板防水、防污且易于维护。 这种类型的乙烯基地板最适合容易磨损的高流量区域。

Water-resistant, scratch-resistant vinyl flooring from us will make your life effortless. Today’s vinyl flooring is long-lasting, low-maintenance, and simple to install. We have it all, whether you need luxury vinyl plank flooring, vinyl tile, or vinyl sheet flooring. Our range of the most popular styles, colours, textures, and top brands is unrivaled. You’ll want to put in every room of your house because of its financial benefits.
Vinyl Flooring Dubai
Vinyl Flooring Dubai offers the best installation services at your doorstep for all of your flooring requirements. We offer the best vinyl flooring, hardwood flooring, and various other flooring options for any commercial or residential setting, such as homes, offices, hotels, schools, restaurants, etc. Our collection includes:

Vinyl tiles Vinyl sheet Flooring Floor skirting Customized orders

We are a high-quality combination of design, durability, and long-term guarantee, all at an affordable price.

Vinyl flooring is a floor covering made of 4 layers of materials. The first, or bottom, layer is the backing layer, which is often constructed of cork or foam. It is intended to act as an underlayment for vinyl flooring, eliminating the need to install another material before laying it down. It also serves as a cushion to make walking on the floor more pleasant and a sound barrier to keep noise at bay.

Types of Vinyl Flooring
Vinyl Flooring

Vinyl tiles are made to look like stone or ceramic tiles. They, like vinyl planks, are available in a wide range of designs and colours that mirror their natural counterparts. Some individuals use grout when installing vinyl tiles to approximate the look of stone or ceramic tiles more accurately. Many individuals like vinyl tiles for small portions of their home because, unlike stone tiles, vinyl tiles can be easily trimmed to fit into small spaces.

Vinyl Sheet

Unlike vinyl planks and tiles, vinyl sheets are available in rolls up to twelve feet wide and may be installed in a single step. The preferable choice is a vinyl sheet because of its affordability and endurance.

Vinyl Plank

Vinyl planks are designed to provide the appearance of hardwood flooring and are available in a variety of wooden patterns. Many people choose vinyl planks over wood, as vinyl planks are water-resistant, stain-resistant, and simple to maintain. This type of vinyl flooring is best suited for high-traffic areas prone to wear and tear.

item.title: 如何用正确的维生素和蛋白质锻炼肌肉 | How to Build Muscle With the Right Vitamins and Proteins
item.description:
拉米鲁法伊

发表于如果你想锻炼肌肉，你需要确保摄入足够的蛋白质和维生素。 蛋白质对于肌肉生长至关重要，而维生素有助于您的身体更好地吸收和利用蛋白质。 在这篇博文中，我们将向您展示蛋白质和维生素对肌肉生长的重要性，如何将它们纳入您的饮食，以及您可以从中获得的好处。

阅读更多 锻炼肌肉的最佳方法。

蛋白质和维生素对肌肉生长的重要性。

它是肌肉组织的主要组成部分，是修复和维持肌肉细胞所必需的。 蛋白质摄入量应根据您的个人需求量身定制，但通常，较高的蛋白质摄入量与增加的肌肉质量和力量有关 (1,2)。

蛋白质的来源很多，包括以动物为基础的食物，如肉类、家禽、鱼和蛋，以及以植物为基础的来源，如豆类、豆类、坚果和种子。 一般来说，动物蛋白被认为质量更高，因为它们含有肌肉生长所需的所有必需氨基酸 (3)。 然而，如果将植物蛋白结合得当，它们也可以成为极好的蛋白质来源。 例如，大米和豆类一起食用时可提供所有必需氨基酸 (4)。

阅读更多 锻炼肌肉的最佳方法。

维生素

维生素是微量营养素，在体内许多生化反应中起着至关重要的作用。 它们是最佳健康和幸福所必需的，并且可以根据它们在体内的水平对肌肉生长产生积极或消极的影响 (5)。 维生素有两种主要类型：水溶性和脂溶性。 水溶性维生素包括维生素 C 和 B 族维生素（硫胺素、核黄素、烟酸、泛酸、生物素、叶酸、维生素 B6、维生素 B12）。 这些维生素溶于水，不被人体储存； 因此，它们需要每天食用。 脂溶性维生素包括维生素A、D、E和K。这些维生素溶解在脂肪中，可以被人体储存； 但是，如果过量食用，它们也可能达到毒性水平 (6)。 重要的是要食用各种不同的食物，以获得适当健康和肌肉生长所需的所有营养。 然而，有些人可能无法仅从饮食中获得所需的所有营养，可能需要补充。 对于有特定营养缺乏或难以从食物中吸收营养的人来说尤其如此 (7)。 阅读更多 锻炼肌肉的最佳方法。 在饮食中加入蛋白质和维生素时，第一步是制定膳食计划。 这将帮助您确保获得每天所需的所有营养。 制定膳食计划时需要牢记以下几点： • 确保每餐都包含富含蛋白质的食物。 蛋白质的良好来源包括瘦肉、鱼、家禽、鸡蛋、奶制品、豆类和坚果。 这些提供肌肉生长所需的必需维生素和矿物质。 健康脂肪的良好来源包括橄榄油、鳄梨和坚果。 阅读更多 锻炼肌肉的最佳方法。

除了吃

健康饮食，您可能还需要考虑在饮食中补充蛋白质粉或氨基酸。 如果您无法仅通过饮食获得足够的蛋白质，这将特别有益。 蛋白粉可以与水或牛奶混合，在锻炼之前或之后服用。 氨基酸也可以在锻炼之前或之后服用。

用餐时间。

在尝试增加肌肉质量时，注意进餐时间也很重要。 锻炼前后最好吃富含蛋白质和碳水化合物的食物。 这将帮助您的身体从锻炼中恢复并更有效地建立肌肉组织。

阅读更多 锻炼肌肉的最佳方法。

蛋白质和维生素对肌肉生长的好处。

蛋白质和维生素对肌肉生长的主要好处之一是改善肌肉恢复。 当你锻炼时，你的肌肉会被分解，需要时间来修复并重新变得更强壮。 蛋白质提供了这个过程所需的构建块，而维生素有助于促进肌肉恢复过程中发生的许多生化反应。
改善肌肉恢复意味着您将能够更频繁地去健身房并更加努力地训练，从而随着时间的推移增加肌肉质量。 锻炼后您的酸痛感也会减少，因此您可以保持活跃并不受干扰地享受您最喜爱的活动。

阅读更多 锻炼肌肉的最佳方法。

增加肌肉质量。

除了改善肌肉恢复外，蛋白质和维生素还可以促进肌肉质量的增加。 这是因为它们可以帮助您的身体合成新的肌肉组织，并防止现有肌肉组织的分解。
蛋白质对于那些希望获得瘦体重的人来说尤其重要，因为它提供了组织生长所需的卡路里和营养。 维生素在调节新陈代谢和激素功能方面发挥作用，这两者对于优化肌肉发育都是必不可少的。

阅读更多 锻炼肌肉的最佳方法。

增强的性能。

最后，蛋白质和维生素还可以通过提供峰值体力输出所需的能量和营养来提高表现。 无论您是运动员还是只想从锻炼中获得最大收益的人，这些营养素都将帮助您实现目标。
维生素 B 和 C 对能量产生特别重要，而维生素 D 有助于调节血液中的钙水平，这对于适当的肌肉收缩是必需的。 蛋白质提供了一种缓慢释放的能量来源，即使在最剧烈的运动过程中也能维持您的努力。

阅读更多 锻炼肌肉的最佳方法。

结论

蛋白质和维生素对肌肉生长至关重要。 没有足够量的蛋白质和特定的维生素，就不可能锻炼肌肉。 确保您获得足够蛋白质和维生素的最佳方法是制定一个包含这两种营养素的健康来源的膳食计划。 用蛋白质奶昔或维生素丸补充您的饮食也可以帮助您实现目标。 最终，在您的饮食中加入蛋白质和维生素将改善肌肉恢复、增加肌肉质量并提高表现。

阅读更多 锻炼肌肉的最佳方法。
